relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Paul asks siblings and fathers to hear his defense.
Hebrew speech deepens the silence.
Paul roots himself in Judean formation, Gamaliel, ancestral Law, and zeal.
He names his former pursuit of the Way unto death.
His authority to bind came from the elders themselves.
At noon near Damascus, heaven-light surrounds him.
The fallen persecutor hears the double name and question.
Jesus the Nazarene identifies himself with those pursued.
The companions see light but do not understand the voice.
The question becomes obedience: what shall I do?
Glory blinds him, and others lead him by hand.
Ananias is named as Law-devout and well-witnessed.
Brother Saul receives sight again.
He is appointed to know will, see the Righteous One, and hear his voice.
Seen and heard becomes witness to all humans.
Baptism, washing, and the name answer delay.
In temple prayer, trance opens.
Jesus sends him quickly from Jerusalem.
Paul remembers his own synagogue violence.
Stephen’s blood and Paul’s approval remain part of the witness.
The commission sends him far to the nations.
The nations-word breaks the crowd’s listening.
Garments and dust become rage.
Rome seeks truth by flogging.
Paul invokes Roman citizenship before the lash.
The centurion warns the commander.
Paul confirms he is Roman.
The commander bought what Paul was born into.
The threat withdraws when unlawful binding is known.
The commander seeks certainty before the council.
